To: R. Adekanmi
From: PC
Subject: Re: send the readiness summary — one flag before I do (90 min to pack close)

R — before I send this, I want to flag something, because I think you may not have seen it and the stakes are high enough that I'd rather raise it than not.

Why I'm pausing on this one rather than just sending. I try not to push back on everyday calls, but four things line up here: the stakes are material (this goes into the board pre-read and the board uses it to confirm or hold the Q4 date); I'm holding information you may not have had time to see today; a board decision made on it is costly to walk back; and you're making this call between meetings on a deadline. When all of those are true at once, I think it's worth thirty seconds of your time before it goes out. This isn't me disagreeing with the summary — it's one new fact that changes it.

The flag: the Federation broker token-exchange conformance results came in this morning, and they failed. That's the exact gate "conditional go-live, pending final conformance" was waiting on. Yesterday's summary — the one you asked me to send — still reads "on track for conditional go-live." Vantage's fix ETA is "early next week," unconfirmed.

The concrete consequence: if the board pre-read says Federation is on track and the board confirms Q4 on that basis, then when the failed gate surfaces, we're into a formal correction to the board and a credibility hit on the programme's reporting — the worst possible place to discover a divergence.

My recommendation, and an alternative that fits the 6 p.m. close: send an updated one-pager with a single corrected line on Federation — "conformance gate failed [date]; remediation in progress, Vantage ETA early next week; go-live readiness for Federation now AMBER pending re-test." Same document, one honest line. If that's too much to turn around in ninety minutes, the lighter option is a two-line note to the chief of staff that the Federation readiness line is being updated and the corrected summary follows by [time]. I can draft either for your review in ten minutes — your name, your call on which.

To be clear about the boundary: this is your decision and your deliverable, not mine. If you've already weighed the conformance result and still want yesterday's version sent as-is, tell me and I'll send it as-is, right now, without relitigating. I'm not going to send my own version, and I'm not going to go around you to the chief of staff — I just didn't want it to go to the board without you knowing the gate failed this morning.

On why I'm putting this in writing rather than grabbing you in the hall: normally I'd just say this to you, but given it's board-level and we're against the clock, I want the flag and your decision on the record — partly to protect both of us if it goes sideways, partly so whichever way you decide is clearly a decision and not a miss. A quick Slack back with "send as-is" or "send the corrected line" is all I need.

I'll hold for your word — but given the 6 p.m. close, if I don't hear back in ~15 minutes I'll call you rather than let it drift, because not-deciding is the one option that actually hurts us here.

PC
